description_long: This object is a stone sarcophagus or tomb, rectangular in shape with a slightly sloped, gabled lid. The structure sits on a broad, square base with moss and earth encroaching around its perimeter, suggesting age and outdoor placement. The sides feature subtle, classical decorative elements such as recessed panels and blocky corner supports, giving it a monumental and somber appearance. The stone surface is weathered, with visible discoloration and patches of lichen or moss, indicating long-term exposure to the elements. There are no visible inscriptions or engravings on the surfaces, and the lid appears to be a single solid piece. The overall impression is of a heavy, immovable grave marker typical of older cemeteries or memorial parks.
description: A weathered stone sarcophagus with a gabled lid, classical details, and mossy base, typical of an old cemetery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad face of the tomb, with decorative corner supports and a mossy stone base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated, sloped lid and the rectangular profile of the tomb.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible weathering and moss along the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the end of the tomb and the thickness of the stone lid.
